hypothesis: Action coupling produces a reproducible discontinuous transition and matched-budget forward/reverse hysteresis that survives washout controls.

controls.description: Each phase-map coupling uses identical seeded data and initialization within an architecture/seed cell.
Every path stage restarts from the identical seeded initialization with the same target-update budget.
Forward and reverse paths receive the same neutral washout and target-update budgets at each stage.

gates.criterion: A segmented discontinuity beats the best smooth null by at least 10% held-out MSE for at least two order parameters; critical estimates replicate within 20% across two architectures and five seeds; at least two order parameters co-locate within one grid step.

Seed-clustered paired forward/reverse bootstrap 95% intervals, preserving both architectures within seed, exclude zero at at least two contiguous couplings; loop area is at least 0.02, the effect survives symmetric washout, and reinitialization is clear.
